bead.title: Detect Node-wrapped Gemini provider children in running guard
bead.description:
PROBLEM
During the DDx dogfood run for ddx-6f25c87f, the active route was Claude/Sonnet and `ddx work status --json` reported only the Claude provider child, but the worker also spawned non-route Gemini Node wrapper processes such as `node /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/gemini`. The running provider guard did not report or reap those children, so an unavailable or misconfigured non-route provider can keep running outside the selected route. This is a core DDx/FIZ reliability failure: DDx is the surface that must keep work progressing despite provider availability and configuration variance.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/provider_children.go:68-78` classifies provider children only by `filepath.Base(parts[0])`. For Gemini installed through the Node wrapper, argv[0] is `node` and argv[1] is the `gemini` CLI path, so `providerForCommand` returns empty. Existing tests at `cli/internal/agent/provider_children_test.go:198-240` cover direct executable names (`gemini`) but not `node .../gemini`, and the process-group test at `cli/internal/agent/provider_children_test.go:242-292` uses argv[0]=gemini, so it also misses the real wrapper shape.

PROPOSED FIX
Teach provider child classification to recognize known provider CLI paths passed as wrapper script arguments, starting with Node-wrapped Gemini (`node /path/to/gemini ...`). Make the running provider guard and `ddx work status --json` treat those processes as provider=`gemini`, non-route when the active route is Claude, and reap the full process group just like direct Gemini provider children.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- cli/internal/agent/provider_children.go
- cli/internal/agent/provider_children_test.go
- any existing cmd/work status test file needed to assert JSON/status behavior

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change FIZ route selection, provider/model catalogs, or provider availability probing.
- Do not add a separate supervisor or server-managed worker feature in this bead.
- Do not change the active-route preservation behavior for legitimate route-owned Claude children.
- Do not hand-edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl`.
bead.acceptance:
1. `TestProviderForCommandDetectsNodeWrappedGemini` proves `providerForCommand("/path/to/node /path/to/gemini") == "gemini"` while ordinary non-provider Node commands still return empty.
2. `TestRunningProviderGuardReapsNodeWrappedGeminiProcessGroup` starts a fake Node wrapper command whose argv includes a `gemini` path and proves the running guard reports and reaps that Gemini process group while preserving active-route Claude.
3. `TestWorkStatusMarksNodeWrappedGeminiNonRoute` proves `ddx work status --json`/the status rendering path includes the Node-wrapped Gemini child as provider=`gemini`, non_route=true, with a diagnostic when the active route is Claude.
4. Existing direct-provider guard coverage continues to pass, including `TestRunningProviderGuardReapsProviderGrandchildrenByProcessGroup`.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TestProviderForCommandDetectsNodeWrappedGemini|TestRunningProviderGuardReapsNodeWrappedGeminiProcessGroup|TestWorkStatusMarksNodeWrappedGeminiNonRoute|TestRunningProviderGuardReapsProviderGrandchildrenByProcessGroup' -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
